page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for workloads requiring massive memory capacity relative to compute resources. It features 40 vCPUs mapped to 20 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 961 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 24.02 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear trade-off between single-core compute speed and multi-core memory-bound capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 10%, reflecting the lower 2.2 GHz clock speed. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth tests, such as uncached large block reads at 61.46 GB/sec and writes at 29.28 GB/sec, achieve average tier results. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the average tier, processing 21.2 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance represents a specialized hardware class where budget is allocated heavily toward memory density rather than raw compute speed or local storage. It is highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server configured with 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor on the x86_64 architecture. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance allocates its resource profile heavily toward memory capacity, offering 24.02 GB of RAM per core. Benchmark data reveals a trade-off: single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core operations and memory bandwidth achieve average-tier results, including uncached large-block read speeds of 61.46 GB/sec. This specialized hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server featuring 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads like in-memory databases and enterprise caching.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server with 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM, ideal for large in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server delivering massive RAM capacity for in-memory databases and memory-bound enterprise applications.
bullet_points: 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM providing 24.02 GB of memory per core
Powered by a 2.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture
Optimized for memory capacity with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth capabilities
Best suited for large in-memory databases, caching, and ETL pipelines
